title: carex canina subsp. canina
intro: Carex canina subsp. canina, also known as dog's tail grass, is a perennial grass species native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which have been used to treat wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The plant is rich in saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties and provide health benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved immune function, and antioxidant activity. Carex canina subsp. canina can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
